mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightening and rising consumer income, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of instances following a >0.5pp UNRATE drop, or signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer spending capacity and liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply-side drag, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations following an INDPRO decline, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, or signal alpha turns negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms synchronized economic recovery, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal renewed construction activity and household wealth effects, which translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations following HOUST increases, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.
